course_description:
This course enables students to elevate their existing scientific communication skillsets, directly supporting the presentation of original research both in a written and oral presentations. Students will be trained in critical, creative and practical thinking, communicating with expert and lay audiences, project management and data analysis.

Lab coat, tick, safety glasses, tick, licence to perform experiments, tick….. What am I missing?

Being a highly skilled and successful biomedical researcher in today’s world requires far more than scientific knowledge and laboratory skills. You need to be able to (1) identify reliable resources, (2) design appropriate experiments, (3) critique the work of others, (4) effectively communicate to diverse audiences and (5) build collegial and sustainable networks.

In BIOM6070 you will work individually and collaboratively with your peers under the guidance of experienced UQ biomedical researchers. The format for this course includes pre-reading, a series of focused interactive workshops and the delivery of small and large assignments that are designed to support your preparation for Honours and future study/careers.

By the end of this course, you should have the knowledge and skills required to effectively design, execute and disseminate your research findings in an impactful way. Additionally, you will have established a network of peer researchers to carry forward as you advance your careers.

restrictions: Restricted to students enrolled in BBiomedSc(Hons); or BSc(Hons) or BAdvSc(Hons) majoring in Biomedical Science or Cell Biology only.

course_contacts.name: Student and Academic Administration Team
course_contacts.notes: The SBMS Student and Academic Administration Team is located on Level 1 of the Sir William Macgregor Building (64-130).

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
BIOM6070 runs as an intensive program during Weeks 1 - 5 of the semester.
Students will attend in person for two days per week (Tuesday and Wednesday), for two blocks of 3 hours on each day - total of 12 hours per week.
All assessment will be completed within the 5 weeks, except for a Seminar Reflection Report (worth 10%) due in Week 13.

learning_outcomes.description: To understand and apply various software programs for analysis of biomedical literature and data
To create a scientific question and design experiments for testing a hypothesis
To critically evaluate scientific data and previously published work
To communicate research findings through written, oral, poster formats to diverse audiences
To work effectively in a research team, identify the characteristics of effective teamwork and critically evaluate personal and peer contributions to team processes
To evaluate ethical issues in biomedical research and apply culturally inclusive, responsible, and ethical conduct in their research practice

assessment_details.title: Experimental design and research practices test
assessment_details.task_description: For this in-class test, you will be assessed on experimental design concepts and research practices covered in the first 3 weeks of the course. Further assessment details will be provided on Blackboard during the semester. Have your UQ student ID card available for all your exams. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct . Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The test will be undertaken online via the Inspera eAssessment platform. This online Inspera test will comprise both multiple choice and short answer format and will be held during scheduled class time in week 4 of the semester.
Students will be required to bring a laptop to the workshops that meets the device requirements for Inspera. It is important that you check before the workshops that your laptop meets the device requirements for using the Inspera Assessment platform. If you do not own a suitable laptop, you can borrow one from the Library . The Library website Get familiar with Inspera provides the latest information for students about using Inspera. Further assessment details will be provided on Blackboard during the semester.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .
Late submission
No late submissions permitted.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam .

assessment_details.title: Poster Planning, Production and Presentation Assessment
assessment_details.due_date: 24/03/2026 3:00 pm A PDF copy of the final poster must be submitted as per the instructions on Blackboard prior to the final poster oral presentation
assessment_details.task_description: For this assessment, you will create and present a scientific poster that critically evaluates a recent high impact research publication in biomedical science. The task involves identifying a recent research publication in your field, critical evaluation of the data and analyses presented in the publication, generation of a scientific poster summarising the key findings, and presentation of the poster to UQ biomedical science experts. Further assessment details will be provided on Blackboard during the semester. This assessment is marked equally on the final poster PDF (50%) and the oral poster presentation (50%) - total of 50% of final grade You are required to bring photo identification to your presentation. As this assessment is marked by two or more examiners and a post-marking moderation process is applied, re-marks are not permitted. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct . To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
You must submit your Assessment task, with the electronic coversheet available on the School's website (under Undergraduate - Assessment), to Turnitin by the submission deadline. You may submit drafts to Turnitin up to the due date, but you will only be able to submit once on or after the due date. You should also keep an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days as students have been working on this assessment item cumulatively over 5 weeks.
A request for an extension must be submitted through my.UQ as soon as it becomes evident that an extension is needed, but no later than one calendar day after the assessment task submission due date and time.
Requests for extension received more than one calendar day after the assessment task submission due date and time will not be accepted for consideration unless you provide evidence of exceptional circumstances preventing you from submitting a request prior to the deadline, e.g., due to hospitalisation.
If you have been granted an extension, then the assessment specified penalty listed under Late Submission will be applied to submissions made after the due date of the approved extension.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
If you are experiencing technical difficulties uploading your submission, please email a copy of your assessment to sbms@enquire.uq.edu.au so this can be logged on your behalf before the due date.

assessment_details.title: Workshop diary
assessment_details.due_date: 27/02/2026 3:00 pm 6/03/2026 3:00 pm 13/03/2026 3:00 pm 20/03/2026 3:00 pm 27/03/2026 3:00 pm Weekly workshop diary entries are to be submitted on Fridays at the end of each week. assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: For this assessment, you are required to complete a workshop diary entry at the end of each week. The purpose of the diary is to encourage personal reflection on your learnings, consolidate key concepts, and identify areas for further development. For this task you will be prompted with questions to answer relating to the workshop topics and activities. Completion of this task will be assessed at the end of each workshop by your instructors - 5% of total 25% marks are allocated each week. 'Workshop diary' includes reflections throughout the 5 weeks, as well as an assessment of participation and contribution. The final workshop diary will be submitted via Blackboard at the end of week 5. Further assessment details will be provided on Blackboard during the semester. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct . Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Seminar Reflection Report
assessment_details.task_description: For this assessment, you are required to write a brief seminar reflection report on a biomedical science seminar you have attended during the semester. Your report should summarise the seminar's key points and learnings, critical evaluation of the research presented including techniques and innovation, communication and presentation style, as well as relevance to your research area of interest. The report will assess your written communication skills. The report length is maximum of 800 words. Further assessment details will be provided on Blackboard during the semester. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct . To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.topic: Getting to know you and resources for biomedical researchers LEARNING OBJECTIVES: To promote networking and collaboration amongst student cohort To train students to work effectively in teams To familiarise students with relevant software programs to support biomedical research LEARNING ACTIVITIES: SESSION 1A: Course overview; Honours goal setting and myth busters (with panel of recent honours graduates) SESSION 1B: Brief introduction/exercise relating to GraphPad Prism, Endnote, Pubmed and Biorender; team activity exploring other useful resources for biomedical researchers Learning outcomes: L01, L04, L05

Ethics, Integrity and Experimental Design LEARNING OBJECTIVES: To be able to conduct scientific research in an ethical, integral, equitable and inclusive manner To understand the opportunities and responsibilities of using AI To design a scientific question, research hypothesis, experimental aims, and craft an informative abstract LEARNING ACTIVITIES: SESSION 2A: Scientific and Experimental design workshop; Poster presentation introductory activities - Poster Power Hour Showcase SESSION 2B: Ethics and integrity case studies (retraction watch) worksheets/class discussions; AI group task focused on equity and inclusivity in research design; Cultural consideration in biomedical research Learning outcomes: L02, L04, L05, L06

Critical analysis for biomedical scientists LEARNING OBJECTIVES: To critically evaluate scientific data and literature, assessing credibility and reliability To develop skills in formulating and asking questions LEARNING ACTIVITIES: SESSION 3A: Critical analyses of scientific literature workshop and "Figuring out figures" workshop SESSION 3B: Introduction to scientific communication - Written and oral communication workshops and ECR showcase Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

Scientific communication and presentation LEARNING OBJECTIVES: To develop scientific communication skills using various formats, written, oral, poster To hone skills in presenting to various audiences, including lay LEARNING ACTIVITIES: SESSION 4A: How to prepare a scientific proposal and research plan - Mock assessment of exemplar theses in teams and class discussions SESSION 4B: Tailoring your message to match your audience – group communication task tailoring key messages to diverse audiences (government, community, science peers); Mock Poster presentations and feedback *Experimental Design test - Weds at 9 am* Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

Connecting and communicating as biomedical scientists LEARNING OBJECTIVES: To develop SMART skills for research project management To establish an engaging online presence To identify and get the most out of career mentors LEARNING ACTIVITIES: SESSION 5A: Group activity using "house-of-cards" approach to appreciate diversity and challenges in research laboratories; Setting up an engaging LinkedIn or BlueSky account; Q&A with junior and senior research leaders in biomedical science. SESSION 5B: Final Poster Presentation Showcase Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05, L06

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Recording of Teaching Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library .
